        • format_onix_date()
        • parse_onix_date()
    • Module contents

Module contents¶

onixlib — public API.

Minimal usage:

from onixlib import parse, Product, ContributorRole

# Streaming parse
for product in parse("notice.xml"):
    print(product.isbn, product.title)
    if product.author:
        print(product.author.full_name)
    if product.collateral:
        print(product.collateral.description)
    if product.publishing:
        print(product.publishing.publication_date)
    for ps in product.product_supply:
        for price in ps.prices:
            print(price.amount, price.currency)

# Full load
from onixlib import Notice
notice = Notice.parse_full("notice.xml")
print(notice.header.sender_name)

# Build from scratch
product = Product.new(isbn="9782070360024", title="Du côté de chez Swann")
c = product.add_contributor(role=ContributorRole.A01)
c.first_name = "Marcel"
c.last_name  = "PROUST"
print(product.to_xml())
onixlib.Book¶

alias de Product

class onixlib.CollateralDetail(raw)¶

Bases : object

Ergonomic facade over the ONIX CollateralDetail dataclass.

property cover_url: str | None¶

URL of the front cover image (ResourceContentType 01), or None.

Returns the first URL found in the first matching ResourceVersion.

property description: str | None¶

Main product description (TextType 03), or None if absent.

Falls back to the first available text content if type 03 is not found.

property raw: CollateralDetail¶

Returns the underlying xsdata CollateralDetail dataclass.

property supporting_resources: list[tuple[str, list[str]]]¶

All supporting resources as (content_type_code, [url, ...]) tuples.

Content type codes come from List 158. URLs are extracted from ResourceLink elements across all ResourceVersion items.

property text_contents: list[tuple[str, str]]¶

All text contents as (type_code, text) tuples.

Type codes come from List 153. The text may contain XHTML markup.

class onixlib.Contributor(raw=None)¶

Bases : object

Façade ergonomique sur la dataclass ONIX Contributor générée.

property first_name: str¶

Prénom(s) du contributeur (NamesBeforeKey).

property full_name: str¶

prénom + nom de famille.

Type:

Nom complet

property last_name: str¶

Nom de famille principal du contributeur (KeyNames).

property raw: Contributor¶

Retourne la dataclass ONIX sous-jacente.

property role: List17 | None¶

Premier rôle du contributeur (code ONIX List 17, ex. A01 = auteur).

onixlib.ContributorRole¶

alias de List17

class onixlib.DescriptiveDetail(raw)¶

Bases : object

Ergonomic facade over the ONIX DescriptiveDetail dataclass.

add_contributor(role=List17.A01)¶

Append a new contributor with the given role.

Paramètres:

role (List17) – ONIX contributor role code (List17 enum value). Defaults to A01 (written by).

Type renvoyé:

Contributor

Renvoie:

The newly created Contributor facade.

property author: Contributor | None¶

First contributor with role A01 (written by), or None.

property contributors: list[Contributor]¶

All contributors for this product, in ONIX order.

property extents: list[tuple[str, str, str]]¶

List of (extent_type, value, unit) tuples.

All values are ONIX code strings (e.g. extent_type "00" = main content page count from List 23).

property languages: list[tuple[str, str]]¶

List of (role_code, language_code) tuples.

Role codes come from List 22 (e.g. "01" = language of text). Language codes come from List 74 (ISO 639-2/B, e.g. "fre").

property measures: list[tuple[str, str, str]]¶

List of (measure_type, measurement, unit) tuples.

Measure type codes come from List 48 (e.g. "01" = height).

property product_composition: str¶

Product composition code (List 2), e.g. "00" for single-component.

property product_form: str¶

Product form code (List 150), e.g. "BC" for paperback.

property raw: DescriptiveDetail¶

Returns the underlying xsdata DescriptiveDetail dataclass.

property subjects: list[tuple[str, str]]¶

List of (scheme_code, subject_code_or_heading) tuples.

Scheme codes come from List 27. The second element is the SubjectCode when available, otherwise SubjectHeadingText.

property subtitle: str | None¶

Subtitle of the product, or None if absent.

property title: str¶

Main title of the product (TitleType 01, element level 01).

class onixlib.Header(raw)¶

Bases : object

Ergonomic facade over the ONIX Header dataclass.

property addressee_gln: str | None¶

GLN of the addressee, or None if absent.

property addressee_name: str | None¶

Human-readable name of the message addressee, or None if absent.

property message_number: str | None¶

Sequential message number, or None if absent.

classmethod new(sender_name, sent_datetime)¶

Create a minimal Header with the required fields.

Paramètres:

  • sender_name (str) – Human-readable name of the sender organisation.

  • sent_datetime (str) – Timestamp in ONIX format, e.g. "20260428T040029Z".

Type renvoyé:

Header

property raw: Header¶

Returns the underlying xsdata Header dataclass.

property sender_email: str | None¶

Email address of the sender, or None if absent.

property sender_gln: str | None¶

GLN (Global Location Number) of the sender, or None if absent.

property sender_name: str¶

Human-readable name of the message sender.

property sent_datetime: str¶

Send timestamp in ONIX format (e.g. "20260428T040029Z").

class onixlib.Notice(raw)¶

Bases : object

Ergonomic facade over the ONIX Onixmessage dataclass.

add_product(product)¶

Append a product to this notice.

Paramètres:

product (Product) – A Product facade (its underlying raw object will be attached to the message).

Type renvoyé:

None

property header: Header¶

Facade over the <Header> block.

classmethod new(sender_name, sent_datetime, release='3.0')¶

Create a new empty ONIX message.

Paramètres:

  • sender_name (str) – Human-readable name of the sending organisation.

  • sent_datetime (str) – Send timestamp in ONIX format, e.g. "20260428T040029Z".

  • release (str) – ONIX release attribute (default "3.0"). Must match a registered version in onixlib.models.versions.

Type renvoyé:

Notice

classmethod parse_full(source, version='3.0')¶

Load and parse an entire ONIX file into a Notice.

The whole file is loaded into memory at once. Use parse() for large files instead.

Paramètres:

  • source (str | Path | BinaryIO) – Path to the XML file (str or Path), or a binary file object opened for reading.

  • version (str) – ONIX release string (e.g. "3.0"). Defaults to the current default release. Pass the appropriate value when reading files of a different version.

Type renvoyé:

Notice

Renvoie:

A Notice wrapping the parsed Onixmessage.

property products: list[Product]¶

List of all Product facades in this message.

property raw: Onixmessage¶

Returns the underlying xsdata Onixmessage dataclass.

to_xml()¶

Serialize this notice to an ONIX 3.0 XML string.

Type renvoyé:

str

class onixlib.Price(raw)¶

Bases : object

Lightweight facade over one ONIX <Price> element.

property amount: Decimal | None¶

Price amount as Decimal, or None if absent.

property currency: str¶

ISO 4217 currency code (List 96), e.g. "EUR".

property price_type: str¶

Price type code (List 58), e.g. "02" = RRP excluding tax.

property raw: Price¶

Returns the underlying xsdata Price dataclass.

class onixlib.Product(raw)¶

Bases : object

Ergonomic facade over the ONIX Product dataclass.

add_contributor(role=List17.A01)¶

Append a contributor. Delegates to descriptive.

Paramètres:

role (List17) – ONIX contributor role (List17 enum). Defaults to A01.

Type renvoyé:

Contributor

property author: Contributor | None¶

First contributor with role A01 (written by). Delegates to descriptive.

property collateral: CollateralDetail | None¶

Facade over the <CollateralDetail> block, or None.

property contributors: list[Contributor]¶

All contributors. Delegates to descriptive.

property descriptive: DescriptiveDetail | None¶

Facade over the <DescriptiveDetail> block, or None.

property isbn: str | None¶

ISBN-13 (or GTIN-13) of the product, or None if absent.

classmethod new(isbn=None, title=None, product_form='BC', product_composition='00')¶

Create a new Product with sensible defaults.

Paramètres:

  • isbn (str | None) – ISBN-13 (no dashes).

  • title (str | None) – Main title.

  • product_form (str) – ONIX List 150 code, e.g. "BC" (paperback), "BB" (hardback).

  • product_composition (str) – ONIX List 2 code, e.g. "00" (single-component).

Type renvoyé:

Product

property product_supply: list[ProductSupply]¶

List of facades over each <ProductSupply> block.

property publishing: PublishingDetail | None¶

Facade over the <PublishingDetail> block, or None.

property raw: Product¶

Returns the underlying xsdata Product dataclass.

property related_material: RelatedMaterial | None¶

Facade over the <RelatedMaterial> block, or None.

property title: str¶

Main title of the product. Delegates to descriptive.

to_xml()¶

Serialize this product to an ONIX 3.0 XML string.

Type renvoyé:

str

class onixlib.ProductSupply(raw)¶

Bases : object

Ergonomic facade over the ONIX ProductSupply dataclass.

property availability: str | None¶

Availability code from the first supply detail, or None.

property prices: list[Price]¶

All prices across all supply details, flattened.

property raw: ProductSupply¶

Returns the underlying xsdata ProductSupply dataclass.

property supplier_name: str | None¶

Supplier name from the first supply detail, or None.

property supply_details: list[SupplyDetail]¶

All supply details within this ProductSupply block.

class onixlib.PublishingDetail(raw)¶

Bases : object

Ergonomic facade over the ONIX PublishingDetail dataclass.

property imprint_name: str | None¶

Name of the first imprint, or None if absent.

property publication_date: str | None¶

Nominal publication date string (DateRole 01), or None if absent.

The date string is in the format specified by the DateFormat element in the same block (default YYYYMMDD).

property publisher_name: str | None¶

Name of the first publisher, or None if absent.

property publishing_dates: list[tuple[str, str]]¶

All publishing dates as (role_code, date_string) tuples.

Role codes come from List 163.

property publishing_status: str | None¶

Publishing status code (List 64), or None if absent.

Common values: "02" = forthcoming, "04" = active, "07" = out of print.

property raw: PublishingDetail¶

Returns the underlying xsdata PublishingDetail dataclass.

class onixlib.RelatedMaterial(raw)¶

Bases : object

Ergonomic facade over the ONIX RelatedMaterial dataclass.

property raw: RelatedMaterial¶

Returns the underlying xsdata RelatedMaterial dataclass.

property related_products: list[RelatedProduct]¶

All related products in this block.

property related_works: list[RelatedWork]¶

All related works in this block.

class onixlib.RelatedProduct(raw)¶

Bases : object

Lightweight facade over one ONIX <RelatedProduct> element.

property isbn: str | None¶

ISBN-13 of the related product, or None if not available.

property raw: RelatedProduct¶

Returns the underlying xsdata RelatedProduct dataclass.

property relation_codes: list[str]¶

List of product relation codes (List 51).

class onixlib.RelatedWork(raw)¶

Bases : object

Lightweight facade over one ONIX <RelatedWork> element.

property raw: RelatedWork¶

Returns the underlying xsdata RelatedWork dataclass.

property relation_code: str | None¶

Work relation code (List 179), or None if absent.

property work_identifiers: list[tuple[str, str]]¶

List of (idtype_code, idvalue) tuples for this work.

class onixlib.SupplyDetail(raw)¶

Bases : object

Lightweight facade over one ONIX <SupplyDetail> element.

property availability: str | None¶

Product availability code (List 65), or None if absent.

Common values: "20" = available, "40" = not yet available.

property prices: list[Price]¶

All prices for this supply detail.

property raw: SupplyDetail¶

Returns the underlying xsdata SupplyDetail dataclass.

property supplier_name: str | None¶

Name of the supplier, or None if absent.

class onixlib.VersionInfo(release, namespace, module, message_class, product_class)¶

Bases : object

Metadata about a registered ONIX version.

message_class: type[Any]¶
module: ModuleType¶
namespace: str¶
product_class: type[Any]¶
release: str¶
onixlib.available_releases()¶

Return the list of all registered release strings.

Type renvoyé:

list[str]

onixlib.parse(source, version=None)¶

Stream an ONIX file, yielding one Product per <Product>.

Each <Product> element is deserialised independently via xsdata so the entire file is never held in memory at once. This is the recommended approach for large ONIX feeds.

The ONIX version is auto-detected from the release attribute on the root element. Pass version explicitly (e.g. "3.0") to skip detection or to force a specific version.

Paramètres:

  • source (str | Path | BinaryIO) – Path to the XML file (str or Path), or a binary file object opened for reading.

  • version (str | None) – ONIX release string (e.g. "3.0"). When None (default), the version is detected from the release attribute of the root element.

Yields:

A Product facade for every product.

Lève:

KeyError – if the detected or requested version is not registered.

onixlib.register(info)¶

Register an ONIX version.

Paramètres:

info (VersionInfo) – VersionInfo describing the version.

Type renvoyé:

None

onixlib.cli package
